criteria.label: Always write the base case first and verify strict progress toward it.
Translate a problem's recursive structure into a function (tree walk, divide-and-conquer).
criteria.example: `def walk(n): yield n.value; for c in n.children: yield from walk(c)`

criteria.label: Apply `@functools.lru_cache` to convert overlapping recursion from exponential to polynomial.
criteria.example: `@lru_cache(maxsize=None)\ndef fib(n): return n if n<2 else fib(n-1)+fib(n-2)`

criteria.label: Convert deep recursion into an iterative loop with an explicit stack.
criteria.example: Replace `def walk(n): ... walk(c)` with `stack = [root]; while stack: n = stack.pop(); stack.extend(n.children)`

criteria.label: Avoid mutable default arguments; pass per-call state explicitly.
criteria.example: `def f(seen=None):\n    if seen is None: seen = set()`

criteria.label: Recognize when a recursive design should become bottom-up DP for memory or simplicity.
criteria.example: Edit distance: top-down recursion with lru_cache → bottom-up `dp[i][j]` table when iterating in a fixed order is clearer.
